_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-9R Medications — General (Revised 3/31/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-9R: Medications General — MDHHS Approved 3/31/2025
purpose: Establish the general framework for all Michigan MDHHS medication reference protocols. Defines adult vs. pediatric age thresholds, universal contraindications, dosing principles, and order of operations for medication selection and administration.
routes: Varies by medication — see individual medication reference protocols
indications: See individual medication reference protocols (9-10R through 9-45R) for specific indications
contraindications: Universal contraindication applicable to all medications: known hypersensitivity or allergy to the specific medication — always ask about allergies before administration
Individual medication contraindications listed in respective 9-R protocol entries
precautions: Weight-based dosing: use actual or estimated weight; MI-MEDIC (Michigan EMS for Children weight-based dosing system) is the standard reference for pediatric weight-based dosing
When a weight-based dose exceeds the adult dose, use the adult dose as the maximum
Medication protocols apply only in conjunction with MCA-approved treatment protocols — do not administer medications outside the scope of applicable treatment protocols
notes: Michigan MDHHS defines adult as >14 years; pediatric as ≤14 years — this differs from other common definitions
MI-MEDIC is the standard Michigan weight-based pediatric dosing reference
All medications applicable only within MCA-approved treatment protocol scope
Hypersensitivity to the drug is a universal contraindication and is NOT repeated in every individual medication entry — always assume it applies
Order of operation: confirm indication → confirm no contraindication → confirm correct dose for patient weight/age → confirm correct route → administer → document → reassess
